question_text: Ice at 0°C is left in a warm room. The ice melts into water, and then the water slowly evaporates. Which statement correctly describes these changes?

options.label: A
options.text: Both melting and evaporation are irreversible changes

options.label: B
options.text: Melting is reversible but evaporation is irreversible

options.label: C
options.text: Both melting and evaporation are reversible changes

options.label: D
options.text: Neither melting nor evaporation are physical changes

correct_answer: Both melting and evaporation are reversible changes
explanation: Both melting and evaporation are reversible physical changes. Water vapour can condense back to liquid water, and liquid water can freeze back to ice. The substance remains water throughout—only its state changes.
